title: Aira variegata
intro: Aira variegata, also known as variegated hair grass, is a perennial grass species native to Europe and Asia. Traditionally, Aira variegata has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Aira variegata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Aira variegata is commonly used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Aira variegata is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. This classification places Aira variegata in the order Poales, a group of flowering plants that also includes other grasses and related species. Within the family Poaceae, Aira variegata is also assigned to the subfamily Pooideae, which is a large group of cool-season grasses. The genus Aira is further classified within the tribe Aveneae, a group of grasses that are often found in wetland environments. Aira variegata's classification reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations to its native habitats.

Its leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin irritations. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms.
